releases.title: Hearts of Iron IV: Götterdämmerung
responsibilities: Published by Paradox Interactive (©All rights reserved) in 2024
Lead designer and scripter on Austria
Implementation support on Germany and Belgium
Bug fixes and improvements across all country content

video.caption: Feature presentation by Game Dirsctor Peter Nicholson about Austria and its features.

description: My work on Austria consisted of... well... everything regarding Austria really. The entire release cycle of an expansion starts with a expansive research phase that then informs the design throughout. This then moved into an intensive phase of implementation of the design and iteration upon it as feedback came in. I collaborated closely with designers on related countries (Germany and Hungary) as well as QA to ensure the content was always devloping in the expected direction while still being balanced and fun to interact with, be it as a player of the Austrian content, or as a player of any other country interacting with the Austrian AI.

With the responsibility of Austria as a country comes several critical systems such as the so called Anschluss which kicks off, or fails to kick off, the entire game. For more information on individual systems I will recommend a read through the Developer Diary I wrote as release drew near.

Beyond Austria this expansion also saw me become one of the technical go-to people when faced with particularly stubborn bugs, and as such I also took custody over several critical and high maintenance systems making use of inhouse systems with less than ideal documentation. Check out the expansion on Steam.

links.label: Hearts of Iron IV: GötterDämmerung on Steam

releases.title: Hearts of Iron IV: Trial of Allegiance
responsibilities: Published by Paradox Interactive (©All rights reserved) in 2024
Implementation support on Brazil, Argentina and Chile
Bug fixes and improvements across all country content

video.caption: Release trailer for Hearts of Iron IV: Trial of Allegiance

description: Trial of Allegiance was the second expansion I was part of creating at Paradox Interactive. As designs were mostly already done my main duties on this expansion was to implement and iterate upon the initial drafts.

My efforts were mainly focused on the implementation of Brazilian content as to offload the designer in charge, allowing her to take on a more producer-like role and support the rest of the team. I also offered bug fixing support for all countries as allowing the responsible designers to instead focus on design.

links.label: Hearts of Iron IV: Trial of Allegiance on Steam

releases.title: Hearts of Iron IV: No Compromise, No Surrender
responsibilities: Published by Paradox Interactive (©All rights reserved) in 2025
Design of country content for China (PRC)
Bug fixes and improvements across all country content

video.caption: Release trailer for Hearts of Iron IV: No Compromise, No Surrender

description: No Compromise, No Surrender is the fourth expansion I was part of creating at Paradox Interactive. During this expansion my main focus was the design and implementation of the People's Republic of China. Beyond the challenges of researching such a closed off nation I also had to tread a very careful line between propaganda and player expectations. I managed the design process in its' entirety as well as kept in close touch with content creators from the Chinese, the Taiwanese and the Western playerbases to make sure that all content felt enjoyable to them, no matter their personal stance on the geopolitical situation, both current and former, in the region.

Along with design work I helped advice what designers needed from programmers and laid the groundwork for how designers communicate needs and risks to both production and programming. This ensured that risks could be mitigated early and that everyone, early on, shared the same vision of what was actually needed for the design to work, and how we could modify it should time or cost concerns prove insurmountable.

links.label: Hearts of Iron IV: No Compromise, No Surrender on Steam

releases.title: SkillplanAI
responsibilities: Hackathon project created in 2025 with the aid of representatives from Intelliplan
Backend development in C# and Couchbase

video.caption: Unlisted video of my colleague Cristian demoing the application.

description: SkillplanAI was a hackathon project created in 2025 with the aid of representatives from Intelliplan. The project focused on developing a prototype for a recruitment solution, aided by LLMs and the so called ESCO standard to classify skills in only 48 hours. The team consisted of three fullstack students, a Product Manager and the CEO of Intelliplan. My tasks consisted mostly of setting up and ensuring the smoot operation of the backend infrastructure, extracting skills from free form resumes, passing them into our database and then run the AI to match and generate summaries of the candidates that had applied for a specific role.
